channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: California bill would give public university admission priority to slaves' descendants
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — A California lawmaker said he will introduce a bill Monday that would give admission priority to the descendants of slaves at the University of California and California State University, two of the largest public university systems in the nation.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, TRÂN NGUYỄN and OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Ex-Kansas detective accused of sexually assaulting Black women is dead, prosecutors say
item.description: TOPEKA, Kan. (AP) — A white Kansas police detective accused of sexually assaulting Black women and girls — and terrorizing those who tried to fight back — is dead, prosecutors said as his trial was set to begin Monday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN HANNA, HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH and NICK INGRAM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Defense lawyer says veteran 'acted to save' people by using chokehold on erratic subway rider
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A defense lawyer asked jurors to put themselves in the shoes of frightened subway riders as closing arguments began Monday in the trial of a Marine veteran charged with choking an irate, homeless man to death after an outburst on a New York underground train.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: French government faces no-confidence vote over Barnier's austerity budget
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French Prime Minister Michel Barnier is bracing for a no-confidence vote this week, a political reckoning almost certain to topple his fragile government and send shockwaves across the eurozone.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Man recovering after assault over McDonald's sandwich
item.description: "Slammed the window in my face, then told me to come back with a better attitude," Karrigan Norwood said.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Beachy, NewsNation

item.title: Defendant in YSL gang and racketeering trial stabbed as he awaits jury verdict
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — One of two men who was indicted along with rapper Young Thug and is awaiting a jury verdict was stabbed in jail but appeared in court Monday. Attorney Doug Weinstein confirmed that his client, Deamonte Kendrick, who raps as Yak Gotti, was injured Sunday.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: FBI offers $15K reward for Maine teenager last seen more than 2 months ago
item.description: The FBI has teamed up with Maine State Police in an effort to locate teenager Stefanie Damron, who was last seen in late September and has a history of running away.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Estranged husband accused of murder after missing woman's body found
item.description: A man is facing a second-degree murder charge a week after his wife, who filed for divorce in Clackamas County recently, was reported missing.
item.dc:creator: Ariel Salk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DOJ sues group associated with Mormon sect for fences built on Forest Service lands
item.description: The U.S. Attorney's Office is arguing that the Free Land Holders have civilly trespassed and violated the Unlawful Inclosures Act of 1885.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, NewsNation

item.title: Panic among spectators at soccer game kills at least 56 in the West African nation of Guinea
item.description: CONAKRY, Guinea (AP) — Chaos erupted at a soccer game in Guinea after fans protested a referee’s call and thousands of panicked spectators tried to flee the stadium, leaving at least 56 people dead in the West African nation, officials and witnesses said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Ohio schools offer new religious release program through Satanic Temple
item.description: MARYSVILLE, Ohio (WCMH) -- A new religious release program for public school students is entering Marysville schools, and this one emphasizes Satanic studies.
item.dc:creator: Katie Millard, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Most fun cities in U.S. Ranked. Here's where Los Angeles landed
item.description: Do Angelenos have the most fun? Well, it might depend on your idea of a good time. A new study by Wallet Hub ranked Los Angeles 18th on its list of Most Fun Cities in America but the City of Angels did finish first in a couple of key metrics.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KTLA 5

item.title: Intel CEO steps down as chipmaker falters, lays off staff
item.description: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger has stepped down after a three-year stint leading the company, the chipmaker announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Here are the rising Democratic populist leaders to watch
item.description: A group of Democrats on the left are searching for a new standard-bearer to put forward an economic populist agenda for America after extensive losses in the November election.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, NewsNation

item.title: Great Lakes region gets yet more snow after a weekend of snarled Thanksgiving travel
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — Parts of the Great Lakes region saw new snow Monday and faced the prospect of even more this week after U.S. travelers battled harsh weather to get home after Thanksgiving, forecasters said.
item.dc:creator: CARA ANNA, CAROLYN THOMPSON and JONAH BRONSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chainsaw-wielding man killed by police at assisted living facility in Illinois
item.description: The man had gotten into the lobby of the building and started confronting residents with the chainsaw, according to investigators.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, Fox 5

item.title: Meghan McCain rips Hunter Biden 'nepo baby' pardon
item.description: "No one is above the law except the Presidents nepo baby is a helluva parting message for democrats," McCain wrote.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Daniel Penny returns to court for start of closing arguments in subway chokehold trial
item.description: Jurors return to a Manhattan courtroom for closing arguments in the trial against Daniel Penny for the subway chokehold death of Jordan Neely.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cyber Monday shoppers expected to set a record on the year's biggest day for online shopping
item.description: Consumers in the United States are scouring the internet for online deals as they look to take advantage of the post-Thanksgiving shopping marathon with Cyber Monday.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Here are the times that Biden, the White House said they wouldn’t pardon Hunter
item.description: President Biden and the White House have emphatically insisted the president wouldn’t pardon his son, Hunter Biden.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hannah Kobayashi's missed flight was 'intentional': LAPD chief
item.description: Kobayashi disappeared and stopped contacting her family just days after missing a connecting flight at LAX.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden pardons his son Hunter despite previous pledges not to
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden pardoned his son, Hunter, sparing the younger Biden a possible prison sentence for federal felony gun and tax convictions and reversing his past promises not to use the extraordinary powers of the presidency for the benefit of his family.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: GOP's small majority adds to challenge of tax reform
item.description: House Republicans are heading into the new Congress with a lofty set of tax reform goals, an ambitious agenda made more difficult by a razor-thin majority.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Saturn's rings will 'disappear' next year: Here's why
item.description: Saturn is best-known for its rings. But, for a short time next year, you'll hardly be able to see them.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: What the cost-of-living adjustment increase in Social Security means
item.description: A 2.5% cost-of-living adjustment increase will mean an extra $50 a month for most Social Security and SSI recipients.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, KRON4

item.title: What to know about Kash Patel, Trump’s pick to lead the FBI
item.description: Kash Patel has called for radical changes at the FBI and was a fierce and vocal critic of the bureau’s work as it investigated ties between Russia and Donald Trump’s 2016 presidential campaign.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ohio State, Michigan players involved in melee after Buckeyes loss, multiple people pepper sprayed
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (WCMH) — Michigan players planted their team flag at midfield of Ohio Stadium on Saturday afternoon after a 13-10 win over No. 2 Ohio State, leading to a brawl between players from both teams with police attempting to separate the sides and pepper spray being used.
item.dc:creator: Orri Benatar, KTLA 5

item.title: Hidden gems of early Cyber Monday deals
item.description: BestReviews has assembled a collection of top-quality gifts priced under $50, currently available at discounts of up to 60% off.
item.dc:creator: Lizzy Briskin, ABC 4

item.title: Bob Bryar, former My Chemical Romance drummer, dies at 44
item.description: Bob Bryar, the drummer of the rock band My Chemical Romance from 2004 to 2014 has died, TMZ reports. He was 44.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Ratliff, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump tariffs threaten to crack open North American economies
item.description: MEXICO CITY — President-elect Trump’s pledge to impose tariffs on Mexico and Canada threatens to upend decades of North American integration, a bumpy process that’s recast the two U.S. neighbors as providers of raw and finished goods for the world’s biggest economy.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, WGN 9

item.title: USPS pauses int'l shipping to Canada amidst strike
item.description: (KRON) -- At a time when many people are sending gifts far and wide in time for the holiday season, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Tor Smith, KRON4

item.title: Amazon worker strike: 5 things to know
item.description: The strike, dubbed "Make Amazon Pay," has planned demonstrations in more than 20 countries including the United States, and the protests are slated to last through Cyber Monday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, WGN 9

item.title: Cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought banana art for $6.2 million eats the fruit in Hong Kong
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — A cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought a piece of conceptual art consisting of a simple banana, duct-taped to a wall, for $6.2 million last week ate the fruit in Hong Kong on Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4
